post_generation_intervention.analysis_markdown:
# Post-generation intervention report

Library: jline:jline:2.14.6
Stage: metadata_fix_failed

## Summary
The post-generation failure was caused by a single generated native test, `TerminalLineSettingsTest.constructorUsesInheritedInputWhenAConsoleProviderIsAvailable()`. This was **not** a reachability-metadata failure.

The failing assertion was `assertThat(System.console()).isNotNull()`. In the native-image test process, `System.console()` remained `null`, so the test failed before any missing-registration error occurred.

## Root cause
This failure was caused by a runtime/environment assumption in the generated test, not by missing metadata:

- The test assumes the native test executable has an inherited interactive console available.
- The Gradle failure excerpt shows a plain assertion failure, not a `MissingReflectionRegistrationError`, `MissingResourceRegistrationError`, or any other metadata-related error.
- The Codex log shows follow-up attempts to force console-provider access through internal JDK console classes and extra `--add-exports` / `--add-opens` settings. That confirms the problem was tied to console-provider/runtime behavior in native image, not unresolved reachability metadata.

Because this is a non-metadata failure, the correct intervention was to remove the generated test instead of preserving it and asking for more metadata.

## Changes made
Removed the generated test and its test-only scaffolding:

- `tests/src/jline/jline/2.14.6/src/test/java/org/graalvm/jline/TerminalLineSettingsTest.java`
- `tests/src/jline/jline/2.14.6/src/test/resources/junit-platform.properties`
- test-only console access flags that had been added to `tests/src/jline/jline/2.14.6/build.gradle`

No metadata files were modified.

## Why the remaining generated support should be preserved
The rest of the generated support is still valid and useful:

- the remaining generated native tests pass for `jline:jline:2.14.6`
- the preserved tests still exercise real library behavior around resource loading, signal handling, reflective terminal loading, HawtJNI native library loading, and OSv terminal integration
- the current generated metadata support is still needed for those passing scenarios and should not be discarded just because the console-dependent test made an invalid runtime assumption

## Verification
After removing the console-dependent test, the library passed again with:

- `./gradlew test -Pcoordinates=jline:jline:2.14.6`
